calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Nov-08-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ernest Der, via Zoom, for and with Brent Kato, Personal Representative; Christina Gallagher Nelson, via Zoom, for and with David Bartel, Objector; Emilie Calhoun, in court, for and with Verna Shaheen, in court, Trustee of the Glassman Trust.

The court confirms with Ms. Nelson that to her knowledge there has not been a new trust case opened under the name of the Harry Glassman Trust. The court states that it is in the position to take the petition under submission with the intent to grant. Ms. Nelson reports of the pending related civil matter which is set for trial in October of 2023 and of pending discovery and that this matter should be continued pending resolution of the civil matter. Ms. Calhoun presented oral argument to support her position with regard to the instant petition and objection and advises that her client is fully bonded. The court acknowledges the bond issue as stated by Ms. Calhoun and that the bond offers the parties protection. Ms. Nelson advises that she has not seen a bond and that bonds do come with caveats. Mr. Der offers advocacy on behalf of the court's intention to grant the instant petition.

After additional discussion, the matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Cristobal. Court Reporter: Tammy Moon. CSR#: 13184. T: 415.362.4346. E: wresources@uslegalsupport.com

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Aug-08-2022 Continued To Nov-07-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ernest Der, via Zoom, for Brent Kato, Administrator With Will Annexed; Christina Gallagher-Nelson, via Zoom, for David Bartel, Objector; Emilie Calhoun, via Zoom, for Verna Shaheen, Interested Party.

Ms. Nelson offers advocacy for her client's standing as an Objector in this matter. The court and counsel discuss the intent to file a new trust case for Harry Glassman and of the pending civil action against the Trustee of Mr. Glassman's Trust. Ms. Gallagher-Nelson offers her position regarding the pending related civil matter and suggests continuing this matter until the pending civil matter is resolved. Mr. Der advises that his client does not have a standing in the Glassman Trust and that the instant petition is intended to provide distributions and offers advocacy for the court to grant this petition. The court advises that it is reluctant to proceed with this petition until the related civil matter is resolved.

The matter is continued to Nov-07-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Apr-13-2022 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Apr-13-2022 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Mar-16-2022 Continued To Apr-13-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person. An Order to Appear will be issued to Administrator Brent Kato and Attorney Ernest Der to appear on Apr-13-2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration have not been filed. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: G. Bastain. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Dec-22-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ernest Der, Esq., counsel for Brent Kato, Petitioner (appearing by video conference); Christine Gallagher Nelson, Esq., counsel for David Bartel, interested person (appearing by telephone).

The Court notes receipt of Mr. Der's response to the Examiner Notes, questioning the determination of the capacity of the decedent, noting that the Examiner's Notes were requesting factual allegations or evidence of the decedent's capacity as opposed to a legal argument. Mr. Der responds, noting he represented the Conservatee at the time of the execution of the Will and determined that she had capacity to execute the same.
The Court next questions the capacity of Mr. Glassman to nominate a successor Administrator to which Mr. Der and Mr. Kato respond.

The Court expresses concern as to possible conflicts of interest to which Mr. Kato responds.
At the Court's invitation, Ms. Nelson concurs with the conflict of interest concern, also questions the capacity of Mr. Glassman to nominate a successor, albeit her client would prefer Mr. Kato if no family member is to serve, and notes other concerns with respect to outstanding litigation. Ms. Nelson confirms that she has not filed written objections on behalf of her client.

After discussion, the Court determines that Harry Glassman had capacity to nominate a successor administrator. The Court grants the Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed. The Will dated October 21, 2016 is admitted to probate. Brent Kato is appointed the Administrator with Will Annexed of the estate with limited authority under the Independent Administration of Estates Act. Bond is set at $75,000. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for hearing on March 16, 2022 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputy Ruiz; Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)
